title: Merck Completes Acquisition of Cidara Therapeutics
text: Merck completed its acquisition of Cidara Therapeutics in January 2026. The transaction adds CD388, a potentially first-in-class, long-acting antiviral candidate with strain-agnostic properties being evaluated for prevention of symptomatic influenza in certain individuals at high risk of complications. Merck described the deal as part of its science-led business development strategy and said the acquisition strengthens and complements its expanding respiratory portfolio. This news is useful for RAG explanations because Merck faces a long-term need to diversify beyond KEYTRUDA and offset pressure from patent expirations, vaccine demand changes, and competition. Adding a late-stage antiviral candidate gives Merck another possible growth path in infectious disease and respiratory prevention, though the value depends on clinical development, regulatory approval, commercial uptake, and successful integration.
